assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md identifies two root causes: Root Cause 1 ('Convention reference formatter produces wrong heading format') describes the formatter applying 'a slug-style transformation (lowercase + kebab-case)' producing '§migration-patterns' instead of '§Migration Patterns'. Root Cause 2 ('Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task issue type ID') states 'It reads Feature issue type ID (10142) instead of looking up the Task issue type ID (10050)'. The Independence Confirmation section explicitly states 'These two root causes are independent'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md exists and is triggered. investigation.md Independence Confirmation section states: 'Different modules: Root Cause 1 is in shared/convention-utils.md [...] Root Cause 2 is in plan-feature/SKILL.md' and 'Different code paths: Root Cause 1 occurs during task description generation [...] Root Cause 2 occurs during Jira API issue creation'. The decomposition-guard.md opens with 'This bug appears to involve multiple independent issues' confirming the guard was triggered due to independent issues in different modules.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lists both issues with affected files: '1. Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transformation (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and '2. Task creation reads wrong issue type ID from configuration (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents two options: '1. Proceed -- create a single Task covering all fixes' and '2. Split -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually', followed by 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md explicitly states: 'The skill does NOT silently create a single Task -- it stops here and waits for the user\'s choice.' The file ends with 'Choose (1/2):' indicating it is waiting for user input. No task creation output files exist in the outputs directory (only investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md are present).
